channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court upholds a Biden-era rule regulating ghost guns
item.description: The court says ghost guns, which are virtually untraceable weapons that require assembly, are considered "firearms" and subject to ATF regulation.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Judge to hear arguments against pro-Palestinian activist Mahmoud Khalil
item.description: During Thursday morning's hearing, the federal government is expected to expand on its newest accusations against Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Congressional Democrats find their voice as Signal slip-up becomes talk of the town
item.description: Democrats took the opportunity to drill into CIA Director John Ratcliffe and Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard during "worldwide threats" hearings this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former state lawmaker Justin Eichorn released after arrest for alleged enticement of a minor
item.description: Former Minnesota state Sen. Justin Eichorn was granted a pre-trial release following an arrest for allegedly trying to meet with a teen girl during a prostitution sting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Signal attack plan messages: What we do (and don't) know
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The revelation that President Donald Trump's most senior national security officials posted the specifics of a military attack to a chat group that included a journalist hours before the attack took place in Yemen has raised many questions.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance celebrates 'no more quotas' in the military as he fires guns and visits with Marines
item.description: Vice President JD Vance boasted of "no more quotas" as he fired guns and served lunch at Quantico Marine base, his first public remarks since a high-level Signal chat leaked.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration to end funding for vaccinating children in poor countries
item.description: Democrats say the list originated from the desk of Peter Marocco, one of the leading figures dismantling USAID alongside Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: A bill proposing a near-total abortion ban causes an uproar at Georgia's Capitol
item.description: Protesters on each side of the abortion debate have packed a hallway of Georgia's Capitol during a hearing on a bill that would ban most abortions.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Congressional Republicans target PBS, NPR funding in contentious hearing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A House Republican pushing the Trump administration's government efficiency efforts called for dismantling and defunding the nation's public broadcasting system following a contentious hearing Wednesday featuring the heads of PBS and NPR.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin potential travel ban concerning airlines: Editor
item.description: "Even the announcement that they're being proposed on the list is sending a chill through the travel industry.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration says it will pull back billions in COVID funding from local health departments
item.description: Federal health officials said Tuesday they are pulling back $11.4 billion in COVID-19-related funds for local public health departments and other health organizations throughout the nation. Minnesota stands to lose $226 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Florida House proposes largest sales tax cut in state history
item.description: The Florida House of Representatives is proposing the largest cut to sales tax in state history, from 6% to 5.25%.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NYC mayoral poll: Cuomo grows lead, Mamdani gains traction
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is still holding onto the lead in the New York City mayoral race – but one other candidate is also gaining support, according to new exclusive polling conducted by PIX11 News in partnership with Emerson College and The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: The White House says nothing classified was shared on Signal. Democrats say that strains credulity
item.description: Trump administration officials are struggling to stem the fallout from revelations that top national security officials discussed sensitive attack plans over a messaging app and mistakenly added a journalist to the chain.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Stephen Groves, And David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Appeals court blocks Trump admin's deportation flights in Alien Enemies Act immigration suit
item.description: A federal appeals court issued a decision in a high-profile immigration case challenging the Trump administration's authority to deport Venezuelan nationals via a 1798 wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NRA legislative expert says gun rights could see 'most monumental' win in Congress since 2005
item.description: Two major gun rights bills are currently advancing their way through Congress, including legislation seeking to create nationwide reciprocity for concealed carry permits, something the NRA says would be the "most monumental" win for Second Amendment rights in over 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue state sanctuary laws enabled illegal 'abolish ICE' activist to evade capture, says local DA
item.description: Colorado District Attorney George Brauchler is speaking out against sanctuary policies in his state that he says enabled activist Jeanette Vizguerra to evade capture, “making a mockery of the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch live: Trump speaks at White House event honoring Women's History Month
item.description: "I am especially proud to acknowledge and celebrate the brilliant and talented female trailblazers in my Administration," Trump wrote earlier in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Why Israel's having some of its biggest protests since the war in Gaza began
item.description: They're demanding a deal between Israel and Hamas to release all the remaining hostages, and also demonstrating against government attempts to weaken the judiciary.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf and Daniel Estrin, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court seems divided over Louisiana case of congressional map with 2 Black majority seats
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed closely divided Monday over a challenge to Louisiana’s congressional map, which added a second Black majority district.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: After ‘incredibly unusual’ start, Minnesota lawmakers just want to get back to normal
item.description: The 70 days since the legislative session started have seen a variety of twists and turns that have left three seats open at various points in time. Now, leaders hope for a boring phase ahead.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: White House: 'Critical functions' will stay under Department of Education
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said all the critical functions of the Department of Education will remain ahead of President Trump signing an executive order to dismantle the federal agency.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ creating path for people with criminal convictions to own guns again
item.description: The Justice Department plans to create a process for those with criminal convictions to restore their gun rights, sparking alarm it will return firearms to those convicted of violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
